summary: Crisp matrix recording captures an unforgettable Sugaree and a smoking Help‑Slip‑Frank sequence, though the closing with Day Job feels odd.
review: The Dusborne matrix delivers crystal‑clear sound that lets every nuance of the 1985 show shine – from the “best Sugaree ever” to the flawless Help > Slip > Franklin’s medley. Reviewers rave about the clean mix and the removal of a distracting high‑end whistle, but a few are puzzled by the decision to end the set with Day Job.

ai_rating.rationale: All detailed reviews praise both performance and sound quality; recording is described as crisp/excellent, show highlighted by standout songs, yielding a top‑tier rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean mix with no high‑end whistle artifacts; clear balance between board and audience ambience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong, energetic flow through the first and second sets with superb transitions; ending choice (Day Job) leaves some listeners uneasy.
